items.q: Does adding value mean the same as making profit?
items.correct: No — added value is the difference between the selling price and the cost of bought-in materials; profit only emerges after all other costs are also paid.
items.misconception: Added value and profit are identical.
items.random_wrong: Adding value means putting prices up illegally.
items.why: IGCSE students regularly merge the two terms; added value can be high while profit is low.

items.q: Is a partnership always better than being a sole trader?
items.correct: Not always — partnerships add capital, skills and shared workload, but profits are shared, disagreements arise and most partners still face unlimited liability.
items.misconception: Having more owners automatically makes a business stronger.
items.random_wrong: Partnerships are only allowed for law firms.
items.why: Students list benefits without trade-offs; evaluation requires both sides of each ownership type.

items.q: Do business objectives stay the same forever?
items.correct: No — a new business may focus on survival, then shift to growth, profit or market share as it matures, and objectives change again in recessions.
items.misconception: A business sets its objectives once and never changes them.
items.random_wrong: Objectives must be renewed annually with the tax office.
items.why: The dynamic nature of objectives is a frequently tested idea in Understanding Business Activity.

items.q: What does the 'S' in SMART objectives stand for?
items.why: Correct answer: Specific. “Strategic” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which 1970 essay argued the 'social responsibility of business is to increase profits'?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Friedman. “Drucker” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many elements are in the SMART acronym?
items.why: Exact answer: 5 elements. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
